1-1.description: The first Rose Bowl game was played, with Michigan defeating Stanford 49-0.

1-2.description: The last NBA game of the 20th century was played as the league entered a new millennium era.

1-3.description: Babe Ruth was sold from the Boston Red Sox to the New York Yankees for $125,000.

1-4.description: The NFL's first overtime playoff game in 50 years saw the Cardinals beat the Packers 51-45.

1-5.description: The Harlem Globetrotters lost their first game in 2,495 outings.

1-6.description: The Jaguars completed the largest comeback in NFL history, overcoming a 27-0 deficit against the Chargers.

1-7.description: The NHL announced expansion to Atlanta and Long Island, adding the Flames and Islanders.

1-8.description: Damar Hamlin of the Buffalo Bills was released from the hospital after suffering cardiac arrest on the field.

1-9.description: The Florida Gators defeated Ohio State 41-14 to win the BCS National Championship.

1-10.description: The temperature at kickoff for the 'Ice Bowl II' NFC Championship was -9 degrees at Lambeau Field.

1-11.description: Rickey Henderson and Jim Rice were elected to the Baseball Hall of Fame.

1-12.description: Joe Namath guaranteed a Jets victory in Super Bowl III, then delivered a 16-7 upset over the Colts.

1-13.description: Air Florida Flight 90 crashed into the Potomac River, canceling all Washington sports events.

1-14.description: The Houston Texans won their first playoff game in four years under rookie head coach DeMeco Ryans.

1-15.description: The Green Bay Packers defeated the Kansas City Chiefs 35-10 in Super Bowl I.

1-16.description: The New England Patriots defeated the Indianapolis Colts in a divisional playoff, continuing their dynasty run.

1-17.description: A 6.7 magnitude earthquake struck Los Angeles, postponing NBA and NHL games.

1-18.description: The St. Louis Rams defeated the Tampa Bay Buccaneers in the NFC Championship to reach Super Bowl XXXIV.

1-19.description: The infamous 'no-call' pass interference in the NFC Championship helped the Rams beat the Saints in overtime.

1-20.description: President Carter announced the U.S. boycott of the Moscow Olympics in protest of the Soviet invasion of Afghanistan.

1-21.description: The Philadelphia Eagles defeated the Minnesota Vikings 38-7 in the NFC Championship, heading to their first Super Bowl since 2005.

1-22.description: The NBA's first-ever game in a domed stadium was played in the Houston Astrodome.

1-23.description: The Washington Redskins won Super Bowl XXII as Doug Williams became the first Black QB to win the Super Bowl.

1-24.description: Kobe Bryant scored 60 points in his final NBA game, leading the Lakers to a comeback win over the Jazz.

1-25.description: The Oakland Raiders defeated the Philadelphia Eagles 27-10 to win Super Bowl XV.

1-26.description: NBA legend Kobe Bryant, his daughter Gianna, and seven others died in a helicopter crash in Calabasas, California.

1-27.description: The New England Patriots upset the St. Louis Rams 20-17 in Super Bowl XXXVI, beginning the Brady-Belichick dynasty.

1-28.description: The Dodgers signed their lease to play at the Los Angeles Memorial Coliseum after moving from Brooklyn.

1-29.description: Wayne Gretzky's mother-in-law was arrested in connection with a gambling ring, shaking the hockey world.

1-30.description: The St. Louis Rams defeated the Tennessee Titans 23-16 in Super Bowl XXXIV on 'The Tackle' at the one-yard line.

1-31.description: Stephen Curry hit 12 three-pointers in a single game, setting a new NBA record at the time.

2-1.description: The New England Patriots defeated the Carolina Panthers 32-29 in Super Bowl XXXVIII, with Tom Brady winning his second MVP.

2-2.description: The New York Giants completed the greatest upset in Super Bowl history, beating the undefeated Patriots 17-14.

2-3.description: The Super Bowl XLVII 'Blackout Bowl' saw a 34-minute power outage in the Superdome.

2-4.description: The Philadelphia Eagles won their first Super Bowl, defeating the Patriots 41-33 in Super Bowl LII with the 'Philly Special.'

2-5.description: Michael Jordan won the Slam Dunk Contest with a perfect score of 50 on his iconic free throw line dunk.

2-6.description: The Green Bay Packers defeated the Pittsburgh Steelers 31-25 in Super Bowl XLV, Aaron Rodgers named MVP.

2-7.description: Tom Brady won his seventh Super Bowl ring as the Tampa Bay Buccaneers defeated the Kansas City Chiefs 31-9.

2-8.description: Spud Webb, at 5'7", won the NBA Slam Dunk Contest, defeating teammate Dominique Wilkins.

2-9.description: Satchel Paige became the first Negro Leagues player elected to the Baseball Hall of Fame.

2-10.description: Magic Johnson returned to play in the NBA All-Star Game after his HIV announcement, scoring 25 points and winning MVP.

2-11.description: Buster Douglas knocked out Mike Tyson in Tokyo, one of the biggest upsets in sports history.

2-12.description: The Kansas City Chiefs defeated the Philadelphia Eagles 38-35 in Super Bowl LVII, Patrick Mahomes named MVP.

2-13.description: The Los Angeles Rams defeated the Cincinnati Bengals 23-20 to win Super Bowl LVI in their home stadium.

2-14.description: The NHL officially canceled the 2004-05 season due to the lockout, the first major pro sports league to lose an entire season.

2-15.description: LeBron James scored 30+ points in his sixth consecutive game during his historic streak with the Miami Heat.

2-16.description: The Daytona 500 featured a last-lap crash that sent Ryan Newman's car airborne in a terrifying finish.

2-17.description: Michael Jordan made his return to basketball, ending his first retirement with the famous 'I'm back' fax.

2-18.description: Dale Earnhardt Sr. was killed in a last-lap crash at the Daytona 500, shocking the racing world.

2-19.description: LeBron James became the NBA's all-time leading scorer, surpassing Kareem Abdul-Jabbar's 38,387 career points.

2-20.description: The NBA held its first-ever All-Star Weekend three-point shootout, won by Larry Bird.

2-21.description: Sidney Crosby scored the overtime goal to give Canada the gold medal in hockey at the Vancouver Olympics.

2-22.description: The 'Miracle on Ice' — the U.S. Olympic hockey team defeated the Soviet Union 4-3 at Lake Placid.

2-23.description: MLB announced rule changes including pace-of-play modifications and video replay expansions.

2-24.description: Jason Collins became the first openly gay athlete to play in a major North American professional sport.

2-25.description: Cassius Clay (Muhammad Ali) defeated Sonny Liston to become heavyweight champion at age 22.

2-26.description: The NBA Board of Governors approved the expansion of the league to include the Toronto Raptors and Vancouver Grizzlies.

2-27.description: Bryce Harper signed a record-breaking 13-year, $330 million contract with the Philadelphia Phillies.

2-28.description: John Scott, an enforcer voted into the All-Star Game by fans as a joke, was named the game's MVP.

2-29.description: On this rare leap day, MLB spring training games continued as COVID-19 concerns began to grow worldwide.

3-1.description: Wilt Chamberlain scored 100 points in a single game for the Philadelphia Warriors against the New York Knicks.

3-2.description: The first NBA All-Star Game was played at Boston Garden, with the East defeating the West 111-94.

3-3.description: MLB announced its new steroid testing policy with suspensions for first-time offenders.

3-4.description: Russell Westbrook recorded his 31st triple-double of the season, on pace to average a triple-double.

3-5.description: The NHL announced it would expand from 6 to 12 teams, doubling the size of the league.

3-6.description: Shaquille O'Neal pulled down a shot clock during a dunk, leading to breakaway rims league-wide.

3-7.description: Barry Bonds hit his 660th career home run, passing Willie Mays for third on the all-time list.

3-8.description: The 'Fight of the Century' — Muhammad Ali vs. Joe Frazier at Madison Square Garden. Frazier won by decision.

3-9.description: Rudy Gobert tested positive for COVID-19, leading to the immediate suspension of the NBA season.

3-10.description: Mario Lemieux played in his final NHL All-Star Game, receiving a standing ovation from fans.

3-11.description: The WHO declared COVID-19 a global pandemic, and the NCAA canceled March Madness for the first time ever.

3-12.description: MLB suspended spring training and delayed Opening Day due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

3-13.description: The NBA celebrated its 50th anniversary season with the unveiling of its 50 Greatest Players.

3-14.description: Dirk Nowitzki passed Wilt Chamberlain for sixth on the NBA's all-time scoring list.

3-15.description: The Cincinnati Red Stockings became the first openly professional baseball team.

3-16.description: UMBC became the first 16-seed to defeat a 1-seed in March Madness history, stunning Virginia 74-54.

3-17.description: Jimmer Fredette scored 52 points in a March Madness game for BYU, captivating the nation with 'Jimmermania.'

3-18.description: Michael Jordan announced his return to basketball with a two-word press release: 'I'm back.'

3-19.description: George Mason reached the Final Four as an 11-seed, one of the biggest Cinderella runs in March Madness history.

3-20.description: Saint Peter's became the third 15-seed ever to reach the Sweet 16, led by head coach Shaheen Holloway.

3-21.description: Kenny Sailors popularized the jump shot in the NCAA Tournament, changing basketball forever.

3-22.description: Zion Williamson's shoe exploded during a Duke game against UNC, sparking a nationwide Nike controversy.

3-23.description: Congress held hearings on steroid use in baseball, with Mark McGwire famously refusing to discuss the past.

3-24.description: Stephen Davidson's Kansas Jayhawks defeated Davidson's Stephen Curry in the Elite Eight.

3-25.description: Sugar Ray Robinson defeated Carmen Basilio to reclaim the middleweight championship for the fifth time.

3-26.description: Magic Johnson and Larry Bird faced off in the NCAA Championship game, beginning their legendary rivalry.

3-27.description: The NFL announced the 'Bountygate' suspensions for the New Orleans Saints coaching staff.

3-28.description: Loyola Marymount's Hank Gathers collapsed and died during a West Coast Conference tournament game.

3-29.description: Michael Jordan hit the game-winning shot for North Carolina to beat Georgetown in the NCAA Championship.

3-30.description: Syracuse won the NCAA Championship behind Carmelo Anthony's 20 points and 10 rebounds as a freshman.

3-31.description: The regular baseball season opened in Japan for the first time as the Cubs played the Mets in Tokyo.

4-1.description: Villanova shot 78.6% from the field to upset Georgetown 66-64 in the NCAA Championship, the greatest title game performance ever.

4-2.description: Villanova defeated Michigan 79-62 to win the NCAA Championship, Donte DiVincenzo scoring 31 off the bench.

4-3.description: Georgetown's Patrick Ewing led the Hoyas to their only NCAA Championship, defeating Houston 84-75.

4-4.description: Villanova's Kris Jenkins hit a buzzer-beating three-pointer to defeat North Carolina 77-74 in the NCAA Championship.

4-5.description: Duke defeated Butler 61-59 in the NCAA Championship after Gordon Hayward's half-court buzzer-beater barely missed.

4-6.description: Duke defeated Michigan's Fab Five 71-51 to win back-to-back NCAA Championships.

4-7.description: The Mighty Ducks of Anaheim clinched their first-ever playoff berth, beginning their Cinderella run to the Stanley Cup Finals.

4-8.description: Hank Aaron hit his 715th career home run, breaking Babe Ruth's all-time record.

4-9.description: The Houston Astrodome hosted its first baseball game, ushering in the era of indoor sports.

4-10.description: The Charlotte Bobcats played their final game of their inaugural NBA season.

4-11.description: The NHL's Winnipeg Jets played their final home game before relocating to become the Phoenix Coyotes.

4-12.description: The first indoor baseball game was played at the Astrodome between the Astros and Yankees.

4-13.description: Tiger Woods won the Masters by 12 strokes at age 21, becoming the youngest Masters champion.

4-14.description: LeBron James suffered a high ankle sprain, missing 20 games in a season that tested the Lakers' depth.

4-15.description: Jackie Robinson made his MLB debut with the Brooklyn Dodgers, breaking baseball's color barrier.

4-16.description: Shea Hillenbrand's bat flip after a home run ignited a bench-clearing brawl.

4-17.description: Ben Wallace and Ron Artest's confrontation foreshadowed the infamous 'Malice at the Palace' later that year.

4-18.description: Damian Lillard hit a 37-foot buzzer-beater to eliminate the Thunder in the first round, waving goodbye to OKC.

4-19.description: Pope Benedict XVI's election briefly delayed NBA games on the East Coast as networks cut to Vatican coverage.

4-20.description: Michael Jordan scored 63 points against the Boston Celtics in the playoffs, still a postseason record.

4-21.description: Kawhi Leonard's buzzer-beater bounced four times on the rim before going in, eliminating the 76ers in Game 7.

4-22.description: The NFL held its first-ever virtual draft due to COVID-19, with Joe Burrow selected first overall by the Bengals.

4-23.description: The Minneapolis Lakers defeated the Syracuse Nationals to win the first NBA Championship.

4-24.description: The MLB held its first amateur draft, changing how teams acquired young talent forever.

4-25.description: The NBA and ABA announced their merger, bringing four ABA teams into the NBA.

4-26.description: The Dallas Cowboys selected Troy Aikman with the first overall pick in the NFL Draft, beginning the '90s dynasty.

4-27.description: The Cleveland Browns selected Baker Mayfield first overall, ending their long search for a franchise quarterback.

4-28.description: Cam Newton was selected first overall by the Carolina Panthers in the NFL Draft.

4-29.description: Eli Manning was drafted first overall by the Chargers, then immediately traded to the Giants.

4-30.description: The Warriors completed a 16-1 postseason run, the best in NBA playoff history at the time.

5-1.description: Nolan Ryan threw his seventh career no-hitter at age 44, a record that still stands.

5-2.description: Floyd Mayweather defeated Manny Pacquiao in the 'Fight of the Century' at MGM Grand.

5-3.description: Pat Tillman's memorial service was held after the former NFL player was killed in Afghanistan.

5-4.description: The Kentucky Derby winner was disqualified for the first time in 145 years.

5-5.description: The Montreal Expos had the best record in baseball when the players' strike began, ending their season.

5-6.description: Roger Bannister broke the four-minute mile barrier, running 3:59.4 in Oxford, England.

5-7.description: The Los Angeles Lakers completed a 33-game winning streak, the longest in major pro sports history.

5-8.description: The Soviet Union announced it would boycott the 1984 Los Angeles Olympics.

5-9.description: Willis Reed limped onto the court for Game 7 of the NBA Finals, inspiring the Knicks to defeat the Lakers.

5-10.description: The Chicago Blackhawks began their Stanley Cup Finals run, ending a 49-year championship drought.

5-11.description: The Warriors set the NBA record for wins in a regular season with 73, surpassing the '95-96 Bulls.

5-12.description: Bobby Orr scored the iconic 'flying goal' in overtime to win the Stanley Cup for the Bruins.

5-13.description: Ja Morant scored 47 points to lead the Grizzlies past the Warriors in the Western Conference semifinals.

5-14.description: The Detroit Red Wings swept the Washington Capitals to win back-to-back Stanley Cups.

5-15.description: Joe DiMaggio began his 56-game hitting streak, a record that has stood for over 80 years.

5-16.description: Michael Jordan's 'Flu Game' — MJ scored 38 points while battling illness in Game 5 of the NBA Finals.

5-17.description: The Phillies retired Mike Schmidt's number 20, honoring the greatest third baseman in baseball history.

5-18.description: Matt Cain threw a perfect game for the Giants against the Astros, the 22nd in MLB history.

5-19.description: Wayne Gretzky played his final NHL game, retiring with 61 records.

5-20.description: The Oklahoma City Thunder reached the NBA Finals for the first time in franchise history.

5-21.description: Allen Iverson stepped over Tyronn Lue after hitting a jumper in Game 1 of the NBA Finals.

5-22.description: The Golden State Warriors eliminated the Oklahoma City Thunder with a historic comeback from 3-1 down in the Western Conference Finals.

5-23.description: LeBron James passed Michael Jordan for most career playoff points in NBA history.

5-24.description: Charles Barkley's 44-point performance led the Suns past the Lakers in the Western Conference Finals.

5-25.description: Muhammad Ali knocked out Sonny Liston in the first round of their rematch with the 'phantom punch.'

5-26.description: Dirk Nowitzki led the Mavericks past the Thunder in the Western Conference Finals, headed to face the Heat.

5-27.description: Reggie Miller scored 8 points in 8.9 seconds against the Knicks in the Eastern Conference semifinals.

5-28.description: LeBron James hit a buzzer-beater over the Raptors, completing a sweep in the Eastern Conference semifinals.

5-29.description: Edmund Hillary and Tenzing Norgay became the first to summit Mount Everest.

5-30.description: The first Indianapolis 500 was held, with Ray Harroun winning at an average speed of 74.6 mph.

5-31.description: The first XFL season ended, with the league folding after just one year.

6-1.description: The Golden State Warriors swept the Washington Bullets to win the NBA Championship.

6-2.description: Babe Ruth played his last game as a professional baseball player.

6-3.description: The Detroit Pistons swept the Lakers 4-0 to win their first NBA Championship, the 'Bad Boys' era.

6-4.description: Armando Galarraga lost a perfect game on an incorrect call with two outs in the ninth inning.

6-5.description: Kevin Durant tore his Achilles in Game 5 of the NBA Finals, a devastating injury during the Warriors-Raptors series.

6-6.description: The Basketball Association of America (later the NBA) was founded in New York City.

6-7.description: Michael Jordan hit 'The Last Shot' over Bryon Russell to win his sixth NBA Championship with the Bulls.

6-8.description: The NFL and AFL announced their merger, creating the modern National Football League.

6-9.description: The Vegas Golden Knights won their first Stanley Cup in just their sixth season of existence.

6-10.description: Dirk Nowitzki and the Dallas Mavericks defeated LeBron James and the Miami Heat to win the NBA Finals.

6-11.description: Michael Jordan's 'Flu Game' — he scored 38 points while battling food poisoning in Game 5 of the NBA Finals.

6-12.description: The Golden State Warriors completed a 16-1 postseason, defeating the Cavaliers in the NBA Finals.

6-13.description: The Chicago Bulls won their fifth NBA Championship in seven years, defeating the Utah Jazz.

6-14.description: The Chicago Bulls won their sixth NBA Championship, completing the second three-peat of the Michael Jordan era.

6-15.description: The San Antonio Spurs dismantled the Miami Heat to win the NBA Finals, ending the Heat's dynasty.

6-16.description: The Cleveland Cavaliers became the first team to overcome a 3-1 deficit in the NBA Finals, defeating the 73-win Warriors.

6-17.description: Ray Allen hit the clutch three-pointer in Game 6 of the NBA Finals to save the Heat's season.

6-18.description: Giannis Antetokounmpo scored 50 points in the NBA Finals closeout game, leading the Bucks to their first title in 50 years.

6-19.description: The Boston Celtics won the NBA Championship in a legendary triple-overtime Game 5 against the Phoenix Suns.

6-20.description: O.J. Simpson led police on a slow-speed chase in a white Ford Bronco, watched by 95 million viewers.

6-21.description: The Golden State Warriors won their first NBA Championship in 40 years, defeating the Cleveland Cavaliers.

6-22.description: The Denver Nuggets won their first NBA Championship in franchise history, led by Nikola Jokic's Finals MVP performance.

6-23.description: LeBron James was selected first overall in the NBA Draft by the Cleveland Cavaliers.

6-24.description: The NHL awarded expansion franchises to Las Vegas, creating the Golden Knights.

6-25.description: Argentina won the FIFA World Cup on home soil, defeating the Netherlands 3-1 in the final.

6-26.description: Zion Williamson was selected first overall by the New Orleans Pelicans in the NBA Draft.

6-27.description: Mike Tyson knocked out Michael Spinks in 91 seconds, the shortest heavyweight title fight in history.

6-28.description: Mike Tyson bit Evander Holyfield's ear during their heavyweight championship rematch.

6-29.description: The first iPhone was released, eventually transforming how fans consume sports.

6-30.description: LeBron James opted out of his contract with the Miami Heat, sparking free agency frenzy.

7-1.description: LeBron James signed his first shoe deal with Nike for $90 million before playing an NBA game.

7-2.description: Andres Escobar was murdered in Colombia after scoring an own goal in the 1994 World Cup.

7-3.description: David Cone threw a perfect game for the Yankees on Yogi Berra Day at Yankee Stadium.

7-4.description: The Higgs boson particle was discovered at CERN, and the sports world took a holiday.

7-5.description: Larry Doby became the first Black player in the American League, debuting for the Cleveland Indians.

7-6.description: The first MLB All-Star Game was played at Comiskey Park in Chicago.

7-7.description: The U.S. Women's National Team won the FIFA Women's World Cup for the fourth time.

7-8.description: The Wall Street Journal published its first edition.

7-9.description: The MLB All-Star Game's 'Ted Williams moment' saw all living legends gather at Fenway Park.

7-10.description: Carl Hubbell struck out five consecutive future Hall of Famers in the All-Star Game.

7-11.description: LeBron James announced 'The Decision' on live TV, choosing to join the Miami Heat.

7-12.description: Disco Demolition Night at Comiskey Park caused a riot, forcing the White Sox to forfeit their second game.

7-13.description: The MLB All-Star Game was played outside the U.S. for the first time, in Montreal's Olympic Stadium.

7-14.description: France won the FIFA World Cup in Russia, defeating Croatia 4-2 in the final.

7-15.description: The MLB All-Star Game's first-ever tie was controversially decided by a Home Run Derby format.

7-16.description: Ted Williams went 4-for-5 in the All-Star Game, including a walk-off three-run homer.

7-17.description: Ty Cobb was inducted into the Baseball Hall of Fame as part of the inaugural class.

7-18.description: Phil Mickelson won his first Masters, ending his long drought at Augusta National.

7-19.description: The 1996 Summer Olympics opened in Atlanta, featuring the legendary Dream Team III.

7-20.description: Apollo 11 landed on the moon. Several MLB games paused to show the landing on stadium screens.

7-21.description: Kawhi Leonard signed with the Clippers along with Paul George in a blockbuster NBA free agency move.

7-22.description: Ichiro Suzuki was traded from the Mariners to the Yankees, shocking the baseball world.

7-23.description: MLB's shortened 60-game season began after months of pandemic negotiations.

7-24.description: The 'Pine Tar Game' — George Brett's go-ahead homer was initially disallowed due to excessive pine tar on his bat.

7-25.description: The Concorde crash near Paris briefly halted international sports travel discussions.

7-26.description: The Dream Team debuted at the Barcelona Olympics, featuring Jordan, Magic, Bird, and more.

7-27.description: A pipe bomb exploded at Centennial Olympic Park during the 1996 Atlanta Olympics.

7-28.description: Pete Rose was permanently banned from baseball for gambling on games.

7-29.description: Nolan Ryan won his 300th game, pitching for the Texas Rangers at age 43.

7-30.description: The WNBA All-Star Game set viewership records as the league's popularity continued to surge.

7-31.description: The MLB trade deadline saw a flurry of blockbuster deals, including the Astros acquiring Zack Greinke.

8-1.description: Barry Bonds hit home run number 756, passing Hank Aaron for the all-time record.

8-2.description: The Dream Team won the Olympic gold medal in Barcelona, defeating Croatia 117-85 in the final.

8-3.description: Usain Bolt won his third consecutive 100m Olympic gold medal at the Rio Olympics.

8-4.description: Tom Seaver won his 300th career game, pitching for the Chicago White Sox.

8-5.description: Marilyn Monroe sang 'Happy Birthday' to President Kennedy at Madison Square Garden.

8-6.description: Cal Ripken Jr. announced he would retire at the end of the season after 21 years with the Orioles.

8-7.description: The U.S. men's basketball team won Olympic gold in Rio, led by Kevin Durant and Carmelo Anthony.

8-8.description: The Beijing Olympics opened with a spectacular ceremony, with Yao Ming carrying China's flag.

8-9.description: Wayne Gretzky was traded from the Edmonton Oilers to the Los Angeles Kings, the biggest trade in sports history.

8-10.description: Alex Rodriguez hit his 661st career home run, tying Willie Mays on the all-time list.

8-11.description: A-Rod began his suspension for his involvement in the Biogenesis performance-enhancing drug scandal.

8-12.description: The MLB players went on strike, eventually leading to the cancellation of the World Series.

8-13.description: The Athens Olympics opened, featuring the U.S. basketball team that would only win bronze.

8-14.description: The NBA resumed its season in the Orlando 'Bubble' after the COVID-19 shutdown.

8-15.description: The Yankees' Old Timers' Day celebrated the franchise's history with former legends.

8-16.description: Cleveland Indians shortstop Ray Chapman was hit by a pitch and died the next day, the only on-field death in MLB history.

8-17.description: A devastating earthquake in Turkey postponed international sporting events.

8-18.description: The Little League World Series continued its tradition as the most-watched amateur sporting event.

8-19.description: Bill Veeck sent 3'7" Eddie Gaedel to bat for the St. Louis Browns, the smallest player in MLB history.

8-20.description: Pete Rose agreed to a lifetime ban from baseball following the gambling investigation.

8-21.description: The Boston Red Sox trailed the Yankees 3-0 in the ALCS. The greatest comeback in baseball history was about to begin.

8-22.description: Michael Vick pleaded guilty to federal dogfighting charges, leading to his suspension from the NFL.

8-23.description: Alex Rodriguez was suspended for 211 games, the longest non-lifetime ban in MLB history.

8-24.description: NBA players boycotted playoff games after the shooting of Jacob Blake, sparking a nationwide conversation about social justice in sports.

8-25.description: Floyd Mayweather defeated Conor McGregor in a boxing-MMA crossover event watched by 4.3 million PPV buyers.

8-26.description: Colin Kaepernick first sat during the national anthem, beginning his protest against racial injustice.

8-27.description: Rickey Henderson stole his 119th base of the season, breaking Lou Brock's single-season record.

8-28.description: The NBA, WNBA, MLB, and MLS all postponed games in support of the player-led social justice boycott.

8-29.description: Hurricane Katrina devastated New Orleans, displacing the Saints and transforming the Superdome into a shelter.

8-30.description: The AL MVP race heated up as Mike Trout and Miguel Cabrera battled for the award.

8-31.description: The Expos played their final home game in Montreal before relocating to become the Washington Nationals.

9-1.description: The NFL season opened with the Indianapolis Colts raising their Super Bowl XLI championship banner.

9-2.description: The NFL cut rosters to 53 players, with multiple surprise cuts across the league.

9-3.description: The NFL regular season kicked off with the defending champion Patriots hosting the Chiefs in a Thursday night opener.

9-4.description: Mark Spitz won his seventh gold medal at the Munich Olympics, setting a record that stood for 36 years.

9-5.description: Palestinian terrorists took Israeli athletes hostage at the Munich Olympics, killing 11 team members.

9-6.description: Cal Ripken Jr. broke Lou Gehrig's consecutive games played record, playing in his 2,131st straight game.

9-7.description: The NFL opened its season with the Baltimore Ravens playing their first-ever game.

9-8.description: Mark McGwire hit his 62nd home run, breaking Roger Maris' single-season record.

9-9.description: The NFL's Week 1 games were played just two days before the September 11 attacks changed everything.

9-10.description: The first NFL games were played on the anniversary of 9/11, with emotional tributes across the league.

9-11.description: The September 11 attacks led to the postponement of all major sports in the United States.

9-12.description: Tom Brady's 'Deflategate' suspension was overturned by a federal judge, allowing him to play.

9-13.description: Sammy Sosa and Mark McGwire's home run chase continued as both sluggers passed 60 homers.

9-14.description: The Houston Astros' sign-stealing scandal was quietly unfolding, later revealed to shake baseball.

9-15.description: Muhammad Ali defeated Leon Spinks to become heavyweight champion for the third time.

9-16.description: The New England Patriots were caught filming opponents' defensive signals in 'Spygate.'

9-17.description: The New Orleans Saints returned to the Superdome for the first time after Hurricane Katrina.

9-18.description: Mark McGwire finished the season with 70 home runs, shattering the single-season record.

9-19.description: Barry Bonds hit his 700th career home run, becoming only the third player to reach the milestone.

9-20.description: Billie Jean King defeated Bobby Riggs in the 'Battle of the Sexes' tennis match, watched by 90 million people.

9-21.description: Professional sports resumed after 9/11, with the NFL returning to play amid heightened security and emotional tributes.

9-22.description: The New York Yankees clinched their division title in the final season at the original Yankee Stadium.

9-23.description: 'Merkle's Boner' — Fred Merkle's baserunning mistake cost the Giants a crucial win against the Cubs.

9-24.description: Ben Johnson tested positive for steroids after winning the 100m at the Seoul Olympics, stripped of gold.

9-25.description: Derek Jeter's final game at Yankee Stadium ended with a walk-off single in the bottom of the ninth.

9-26.description: Australia II won the America's Cup, ending the United States' 132-year winning streak.

9-27.description: The MLB regular season ended with Mark McGwire at 70 HRs and Sammy Sosa at 66, capping the greatest HR race.

9-28.description: The most dramatic final day in MLB history saw the Red Sox and Braves collapse simultaneously, losing their wild card spots.

9-29.description: Willie Mays made 'The Catch' in Game 1 of the World Series against the Cleveland Indians.

9-30.description: Babe Ruth hit his 60th home run, setting a record that stood for 34 years.

10-1.description: Barry Bonds hit his 73rd home run of the season, breaking Mark McGwire's record.

10-2.description: Vin Scully called his final Dodgers game after 67 years, the longest tenure of any broadcaster.

10-3.description: Bobby Thomson hit the 'Shot Heard Round the World,' a walk-off home run to win the pennant for the Giants.

10-4.description: The Brooklyn Dodgers won their first and only World Series, defeating the New York Yankees in seven games.

10-5.description: Barry Bonds hit home runs 71, 72, and 73 to close the season with the all-time single-season record.

10-6.description: Steve Bartman interfered with a foul ball during the NLCS, and the Cubs' curse continued.

10-7.description: The U.S. began military operations in Afghanistan as NFL games continued under heavy security.

10-8.description: Don Larsen pitched the only perfect game in World Series history for the New York Yankees.

10-9.description: The Washington Nationals completed a historic comeback, winning the NLDS after trailing the Dodgers.

10-10.description: The Boston Red Sox began their historic comeback from 0-3 down against the Yankees in the ALCS.

10-11.description: Aaron Boone's walk-off home run in Game 7 of the ALCS sent the Yankees to the World Series over the Red Sox.

10-12.description: The Cubs defeated the Giants in the NLDS, continuing their quest to break the 108-year championship drought.

10-13.description: Bill Mazeroski hit a walk-off home run in Game 7 to win the World Series for the Pirates over the Yankees.

10-14.description: Game 6 of the ALCS — the Red Sox were five outs from elimination before beginning their legendary comeback.

10-15.description: Kirk Gibson hit the iconic walk-off homer in Game 1 of the World Series, limping around the bases for the Dodgers.

10-16.description: The Red Sox completed the greatest comeback in postseason history, winning four straight against the Yankees.

10-17.description: A 6.9 earthquake struck during the World Series at Candlestick Park, postponing the Bay Bridge Series for 10 days.

10-18.description: Reggie Jackson hit three home runs in Game 6 of the World Series, earning the nickname 'Mr. October.'

10-19.description: Curt Schilling pitched the 'Bloody Sock Game' for the Red Sox in Game 6 of the ALCS against the Yankees.

10-20.description: The Red Sox completed their ALCS comeback, advancing to the World Series for the first time since 1986.

10-21.description: The Arizona Diamondbacks and Yankees played an epic World Series, with games becoming a symbol of New York's resilience post-9/11.

10-22.description: Madison Bumgarner threw five innings of relief in Game 7 to seal the World Series for the Giants.

10-23.description: Joe Carter hit a walk-off World Series home run to give the Blue Jays back-to-back championships.

10-24.description: The San Francisco Giants swept the Tigers to win the World Series, their second in three years.

10-25.description: Bill Buckner's error in Game 6 of the World Series let the Mets complete their comeback against the Red Sox.

10-26.description: Game 6 of the World Series — the Cardinals were twice one strike away from elimination before David Freese's heroics.

10-27.description: The Boston Red Sox won the World Series for the first time in 86 years, sweeping the Cardinals.

10-28.description: The Arizona Diamondbacks won the World Series with a walk-off single by Luis Gonzalez off Mariano Rivera.

10-29.description: Game 5 of the World Series — the Cubs lost to the Indians, needing to win two straight to end the drought.

10-30.description: Muhammad Ali defeated George Foreman in the 'Rumble in the Jungle' in Zaire with the rope-a-dope strategy.

10-31.description: The New York Yankees completed a World Series sweep of the Braves, winning their 25th championship.

11-1.description: The Arizona Diamondbacks won the World Series just seven weeks after 9/11, defeating the Yankees in seven games.

11-2.description: The Chicago Cubs won the World Series for the first time in 108 years, defeating the Indians in a thrilling Game 7.

11-3.description: The Houston Astros won their first World Series, defeating the Dodgers in seven games. The sign-stealing scandal would later taint the victory.

11-4.description: Derek Jeter's walk-off homer earned him the nickname 'Mr. November' in the World Series.

11-5.description: The New York Yankees won their 27th World Series, defeating the Philadelphia Phillies.

11-6.description: The Miami Heat extended their winning streak to 27 games, the second-longest in NBA history.

11-7.description: Magic Johnson announced he had tested positive for HIV and would retire from the NBA.

11-8.description: The 'Malice at the Palace' suspensions were finalized, with Ron Artest receiving a season-long ban.

11-9.description: Ezekiel Elliott began serving his six-game suspension after a lengthy legal battle with the NFL.

11-10.description: The first details of the Houston Astros' sign-stealing scandal were reported, rocking baseball.

11-11.description: The Rams and Chiefs played a historic Monday Night Football game, combining for 105 points in a 54-51 thriller.

11-12.description: The NBA fined the Philadelphia 76ers for 'tanking' during their 'Trust the Process' rebuild.

11-13.description: Latrell Sprewell was suspended for choking his coach P.J. Carlesimo during a Golden State Warriors practice.

11-14.description: The Astros fired GM Jeff Luhnow and manager AJ Hinch over the sign-stealing scandal.

11-15.description: Barry Bonds was indicted on perjury and obstruction of justice charges related to the BALCO scandal.

11-16.description: The 'Malice at the Palace' anniversary — Ron Artest had just returned from his record suspension.

11-17.description: Alex Smith made his remarkable comeback, starting for the Washington Football Team just two years after a devastating leg injury.

11-18.description: Dwyane Wade, LeBron James, and Chris Bosh played their final season together on the Miami Heat.

11-19.description: The 'Malice at the Palace' — Ron Artest charged into the stands at the Pistons-Pacers game, triggering the worst brawl in NBA history.

11-20.description: Walter Payton set the single-game rushing record with 275 yards against the Minnesota Vikings.

11-21.description: The NBA suspended nine players following the 'Malice at the Palace,' including Ron Artest for the rest of the season.

11-22.description: Odell Beckham Jr. made 'The Catch' — a one-handed grab against the Cowboys that became the most replayed play in NFL history.

11-23.description: Doug Flutie threw the iconic Hail Mary pass for Boston College against Miami, one of college football's greatest plays.

11-24.description: The Green Bay Packers improved to 11-0, on their way to a 15-1 regular season record.

11-25.description: The Thanksgiving Day NFL games drew over 30 million viewers, reinforcing football's place in American holiday tradition.

11-26.description: The New England Patriots' record 21-game winning streak was snapped by the Indianapolis Colts.

11-27.description: The Auburn Tigers' Cam Newton won the Heisman Trophy amid eligibility controversy.

11-28.description: The NFL Thanksgiving Day tripleheader became the highest-rated broadcast of the holiday.

11-29.description: The Cleveland Cavaliers' LeBron James scored 32 points in his first MSG game, announcing himself on basketball's biggest stage.

11-30.description: The Los Angeles Chargers played in the smallest NFL stadium capacity in decades after their relocation from San Diego.

12-1.description: The Alabama Crimson Tide's dominance continued as they clinched another SEC Championship.

12-2.description: Michigan completed an undefeated regular season and won the Big Ten Championship.

12-3.description: The Cleveland Browns defeated the Los Angeles Rams 30-28 in the NFL Championship Game.

12-4.description: Cam Newton won the Heisman Trophy after leading Auburn to an undefeated season.

12-5.description: Robert Griffin III won the Heisman Trophy, electrifying the college football world.

12-6.description: Lamar Jackson won his second unanimous NFL MVP award for the Baltimore Ravens.

12-7.description: The 'instant replay' was used for the first time during a televised football game.

12-8.description: John Lennon was shot and killed. Monday Night Football announced the tragedy during the broadcast.

12-9.description: The 'Sneakers Game' — the Giants switched to basketball shoes at halftime and rallied to beat the Bears for the NFL Championship.

12-10.description: The first ABA game was played as the league launched its challenge to the NBA.

12-11.description: The longest game in NBA history ended after 4 overtimes, with the Bucks defeating the Sonics.

12-12.description: The Supreme Court decided Bush v. Gore, and the Cowboys-Eagles Monday Night Football game drew record ratings.

12-13.description: Derrick Henry rushed for 250+ yards in back-to-back games, establishing himself as the NFL's most dominant runner.

12-14.description: Tiger Woods announced an indefinite break from golf following revelations about his personal life.

12-15.description: The Cleveland Browns played their final game before relocating to Baltimore to become the Ravens.

12-16.description: The COVID-19 pandemic forced multiple NFL schedule changes and a Week 16 Saturday doubleheader.

12-17.description: The Nuggets-Knicks brawl at MSG resulted in 10 suspensions and a league-wide review of player conduct.

12-18.description: Argentina won the FIFA World Cup in Qatar, with Lionel Messi cementing his GOAT status in the greatest final ever.

12-19.description: Kobe Bryant scored 62 points in three quarters against the Dallas Mavericks.

12-20.description: The Warriors and Cavaliers met for their annual Christmas Day NBA showdown.

12-21.description: The NFL's first Monday Night Football season concluded, having transformed sports television.

12-22.description: LeBron James passed Michael Jordan for fourth on the NBA's all-time scoring list.

12-23.description: The 'Immaculate Reception' — Franco Harris' miraculous catch in the AFC Divisional Playoff for the Steelers.

12-24.description: The NBA's Christmas Day showcase featured five games, drawing massive television audiences.

12-25.description: The NBA Christmas Day games featured Warriors vs. Cavaliers in a Finals rematch, Golden State winning 109-108.

12-26.description: The NBA season continued as LeBron James' rookie year showcased his generational talent.

12-27.description: The 'Greatest Game Ever Played' — the Colts defeated the Giants 23-17 in the NFL Championship in sudden death overtime.

12-28.description: The 'Hail Mary' — Roger Staubach coined the term after his desperation TD pass beat the Vikings in the playoffs.

12-29.description: The Detroit Lions finished 0-16, the first team to go winless in a 16-game NFL season.

12-30.description: The Cleveland Browns finished 0-16, joining the 2008 Lions as the only teams to go winless in the modern era.

12-31.description: The 'Ice Bowl' — the Packers defeated the Cowboys 21-17 in -13°F weather for the NFL Championship at Lambeau Field.
